what_it_does: Defines a React Icons canvas for browsing a categorized SVG icon library, searching and filtering icons, previewing color tones, generating new icon proposals with Vi via window.claude, and adopting, refining, duplicating, or deleting generated icons in window.CV_ICONS.data.
observations: Uses a local ICON_CATEGORIES map to group icon names for the gallery.
Reads icon bodies from window.CV_ICONS.data and renders them as inline SVG with dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
Uses component state for search query, active category, tone, generation panel visibility, prompt text, loading state, proposals, and hover inspection.
Prompts window.claude.complete to return compact JSON for generation, refinement, and duplication workflows.
Generated icons are exposed through parent callbacks addGenerated, updateGenerated, and removeGenerated.
Hovering an icon reveals refine, duplicate, and conditional remove controls.
Search has an empty-state action that moves the query into the generation prompt.
standouts: Combines icon catalog browsing with AI-assisted SVG generation inside the same canvas.
The prompts encode strict icon style constraints: 24 by 24 viewBox, 1.5px stroke, rounded caps and joins, no fills.
Runtime adoption writes new SVG bodies directly into the global icon registry.
The UI supports both modifying an existing icon body and creating a named variant from it.
conventions: Uses aliased React hooks useState_ic and useMemo_ic.
Keeps generated icon persistence outside the component through callback props.
Uses kebab-case icon names in categories, generated proposals, and clipboard copy behavior.
Mixes class-based styling with several inline style objects for small controls.
Uses toast notifications through optional window.dsaToast calls.
Uses global browser objects and app globals directly: window.CV_ICONS, window.claude, navigator.clipboard, prompt, and window.open.
concerns: AI-returned SVG bodies are inserted with dangerouslySetInnerHTML without local sanitization shown in this file.
The component mutates window.CV_ICONS.data directly, so icon registry changes are outside React state ownership.
adopt mutates the proposal object when resolving a naming conflict before updating proposals state.
JSON parsing accepts a bracket or object substring fallback, but there is no schema validation of SVG element types or attributes.
deleteIcon removes any named icon passed to it from the global registry, though the visible delete button is only shown for generated icons.
Several controls depend on browser globals, which makes behavior harder to isolate from this file alone.
notes: This file is an interactive icon-library canvas with a runtime AI workflow for creating and revising SVG glyphs.
questions: Is window.CV_ICONS.data trusted to contain only safe SVG bodies?
Should generated SVG bodies be sanitized before insertion into the registry and DOM?
Are generated icons intended to persist beyond the current session through the parent callbacks?
Should built-in icons be protected from deleteIcon if that function is called from another path?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose, an icon library with real AI generation, matches the component's browsing, generation, adoption, refinement, duplication, and deletion behavior.
apparent_themes: Direct manipulation of a shared icon registry
intention_signals: The file seems intended to let designers inspect the existing ConceptV icon set and extend it in-place while preserving a consistent line-icon style through constrained prompts.
novelty: An icon gallery doubles as an AI SVG authoring surface, with generated proposals that can be immediately adopted into the running icon registry.
contribution: This file contributes a self-contained icon management surface that combines categorized browsing, tone preview, clipboard naming, and AI-driven SVG generation, refinement, duplication, and session removal workflows.
summary_for_embedding: React Icons canvas for a ConceptV design system icon library with categorized search, tone preview, and AI-assisted SVG generation, adoption, refinement, duplication, and deletion.
intention_for_embedding: It was built to make the icon library inspectable and extensible from inside the design-system app while preserving a constrained single-stroke visual language.

symbols.does: Renders the icon library canvas and coordinates browsing, filtering, generation, inspection, and icon management state.
symbols.description: This component owns the UI state for search, category filtering, tone selection, AI generation, proposal display, and hover inspection. It reads icons from window.CV_ICONS.data, combines static categories with session-generated icons, and renders the header, generation panel, toolbar, category bar, icon grids, and empty state.

symbols.does: Requests AI-generated icon proposals from window.claude based on the current generation prompt.
symbols.description: This async function builds a detailed prompt specifying ConceptV SVG style rules and asks for compact JSON containing icon names and SVG bodies. It parses the response directly or from an extracted JSON array, stores up to eight usable proposals, and reports parse or request failures through toast messages.

symbols.does: Adds a generated proposal into the runtime icon registry and parent generated-icon state.
symbols.description: This function ensures a proposal name is present and unique, writes the SVG body into window.CV_ICONS.data, calls addGenerated with the adopted icon, marks the matching proposal as adopted, and shows a toast. It modifies the proposal name when a collision is detected.

symbols.does: Renders a named icon from window.CV_ICONS.data as an inline SVG at the requested size.
symbols.description: This helper looks up an SVG body by name, selects a stroke color from the current tone state, and returns a 24 by 24 SVG with the shared line style. The icon body is injected as inner markup, while fill, stroke width, caps, and joins are set on the SVG wrapper.

symbols.does: Requests an AI revision of an existing icon body and replaces the registry entry with the returned body.
symbols.description: This async function sends the current icon name, SVG body, and requested change to window.claude.complete with instructions to return a JSON object containing a new body. If parsing succeeds, it updates window.CV_ICONS.data and calls updateGenerated for session-generated icons before showing a toast.

symbols.does: Requests an AI-created variant of an existing icon and adds it as a new generated icon.
symbols.description: This async function prompts the AI with a source icon body and a user-supplied variant description, then parses a JSON object containing a new name and SVG body. It resolves name collisions, writes the new body into window.CV_ICONS.data, calls addGenerated, and reports success or failure with toasts.

symbols.does: Removes an icon name from the runtime registry and parent generated-icon state.
symbols.description: This function deletes the named entry from window.CV_ICONS.data, calls removeGenerated if provided, and shows a toast. In the rendered UI, the delete control is only displayed for icons identified as generated in the current session.
